---
title: "Milestone Week-6 Template"
author: "Kirkpatrick & Son Dairy Data"
date: "11 December 2025"

format:
  html:
    logo: logo_b.png   # path to logo file
editor: visual
echo: false
---

# Health Reporting Template

DC305 EVENTS data has been extracted. This report focuses on the EVENTS of LAME and TRIM.

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

## Running Code

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

```{r}
1 + 1
```

You can add options to executable code like this

```{r}
#| echo: false
2 * 2
```

The `echo: false` option disables the printing of code (only output is displayed).

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false

if (!require("pacman")) install.packages("pacman")
pacman::p_load(
  tidyverse,
  dtplyr,
  gt,
  arrow,
  rmarkdown,
  lubridate,
  quarto, 
  arrow,
  zoo
  )

# data you will need for this report
events_formatted <- read_parquet(
  '../data/intermediate_files/events_formatted.parquet')
```


```{r}
#| label: Extract LAME and TRIM
#| 
lame_trim <- events_formatted %>%
  # Create 'lame_trim' to extract these specific events
  filter(lact_group_basic != "Heifer",
         event %in% c("LAME", "TRIM")) %>%
  mutate(year_event = year(date_event),
         floordate_date = floor_date(date_event, "month"),
         floordate_month = month(date_event, label = TRUE, abbr = FALSE),
         locate_qtr = case_when(   # summarize the quarters affected
            nchar(locate_lesion) == 8  ~ "ALL",
            nchar(locate_lesion) == 6  ~ "3Q",
            nchar(locate_lesion) == 4  ~ "2Q",
            nchar(locate_lesion) == 2 ~ locate_lesion, # exactly 2 characters
            TRUE ~ NA_character_  ))     # fallback for missing/other cases)

# unique(lame_trim$lact_group_5)

corrections <- c(     #Correct the multiple entries to group pathology
  "Trim Only" = "Trim Only",
  "Ulcer/Block" = "Ulcer",
  "Abscess" = "Abscess",
  "Abces\\Block" = "Abscess",
  "Ulcer" = "Ulcer",
  "Absce/BlkPoly" = "Abscess",
  "Thin Sole" = "Thin Sole",
  "Injury" = "Injury",
  "Injury/Excede" = "Injury",
  "Injury/Poly" = "Injury",
  "Injury/Block" = "Injury",
  "Ulcer/BlkExce" = "Ulcer",
  "Ulcer/BlkPoly" = "Ulcer",
  "Remove Block" = "Remove Block",
  "Foot Rot/Exce" = "Foot Rot",
  "Foot Rot" = "Foot Rot",
  "FootRot/Poly" = "Foot Rot",
  "Absc/BlkExced" = "Abscess",
  "TR Lame Infec" = "TR Lame Infec",
  "Hairy Wart" = "Hairy Wart"
)

lame_trim <- lame_trim %>%
  mutate(protocols_numbers1 = recode(Protocols, !!!corrections))
```

```{r}
#| label: Graph LAME and TRIM
#| 


# Ensure lact_group_5 has the correct order
lame_trim$lact_group_5 <- factor(
  lame_trim$lact_group_5,
  levels = c("LACT 1", "LACT 2", "LACT 3", "LACT 4", "LACT 5+")
)

lame_trim %>%
  filter(event == "LAME",
         year(floordate_date) >= 2023) %>%   # filter from 2023 to current
  ggplot(aes(x = floordate_date, fill = lact_group)) +
  geom_bar(color = "black") +
  labs(
    x = "Month/Year",
    y = "Case Count",
    title = "LAME Case Counts by Month/Year",
    caption = "All cases by calendar date",
    fill = "Lactation Group"
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ lact_group, ncol = 1, scales = "fixed") +  # vertical panels, shared y-axis
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )

# ==============================================================================
# Stacked bar graph of treatment protocols - ALL treatments
# ==============================================================================

lame_trim %>%
  filter(event == "LAME", 
         year_event %in% c(2024, 2025)) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = floordate_date, fill = protocols_numbers1)) +
  geom_bar(color = "black") +   # adds black border around each stacked cell
  labs(
    x = "Month/Year", 
    y = "Number of Treatments",
    title = "Treatment Protocols by Month/Year",
    caption = "Representation by Time Interval, ALL cases"
  ) +
    scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 180), 
    breaks = seq(0, 180, by = 20),
    labels = paste0(seq(0, 180, by = 20), "")
  ) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)  # centers the title
  ) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "BrBG")       # references the 9-class ColorBrewer BrBG palette
```
